zh.lifeSummary: 當John Richard Day於1418年出生在威爾士丹比爾時，他的父親Morgan Dee28歲，母親Maud Day  Madoc-12歲。他於1451年與Margaret Day Howell在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1515年在威爾士蒙姆爾享年97歲，埋葬在英國波克夏音費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Richard Day于1418年出生在威尔士丹比爾时，他的父亲Morgan Dee28岁，母亲Maud Day  Madoc-12岁。他于1451年与Margaret Day Howell在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1515年在威尔士蒙姆爾享年97岁，埋葬在英国波克夏音費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Day
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English day(e), dey(e) ‘dairyman or dairymaid’. Originally used only of women, it was later used of men with the sense ‘man in charge of the dairy cattle’. This is probably the most common source of the surname.

English: from the Middle English personal name Day(e) or Dey. In western England this is probably a pet form of David , but in northern England and perhaps elsewhere also it is a late Middle English variant of Daw, a pet form of Ralph (see Daw , Dakin ).
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Deaghaidh (see O'Dea ).

variants.name: Goody
meanings: English:
from the Middle English salutation godne dæie ‘good day’, possibly applied as a nickname for someone who frequently used this greeting.
possibly in some instances from Middle English god(e)day(e) (or dey(e)) ‘good dairy man’ (see Day ).

variants.name: Daigh
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Day  .

variants.name: Days
meanings: English (Worcestershire and Warwickshire) and Welsh: variant of Day , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In the West Midlands this name commonly a variant of Dias 2.

variants.name: David
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Daye
meanings: English: variant of   Day  .

variants.name: Daymon
meanings: English (Cornwall): from a variant of the Middle English personal name Dayman, which has a number of explanations. It may be from an Old English Dægmann, perhaps a pet form of names in Dæg- ‘day’. Alternatively, the name may be a pet form of the Middle English personal name Day, to which the hypocoristic suffix -man has been added. Day is sometimes a short form of David but is also a 14th-century variant of Daw , a rhyming pet form of Ralph . The name may also be an occupational name for a dairyman, from Middle English daie ‘man in charge of dairy cattle’ + man ‘servant, worker’, similar in usage to Old English Flotmann ‘sailor’; or an occupational name for a ‘servant of a man named Day’. Daymon is also a variant of Diamond . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

variants.name: Diamond
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): from an Anglicized form of a metonymic occupational name for a diamond worker, derived from German Diamant, French and Dutch diamant ‘diamond’, or Yiddish dime(n)t, from medieval Latin diamas, genitive diamantis, and ultimately from Greek adamas ‘unconquerable’, genitive adamantos, a reference to the hardness of the stone.

English: from the rare Middle English female personal name Diamand, derived from Old French diamaunt, diamaund ‘diamond’, found in Middle English as diamound, deamaunt, and deamon. The name may alternatively be a variant of Daymon or a pet form of the Middle English personal name Day , to which the hypocoristic suffix -man has been added.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Diamáin ‘descendant of Diamán’, earlier Díomá or Déamán, a diminutive of Díoma, itself a pet form of Diarmaid (see McDermott ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Richard Day was born in 1418, in Denbighshire, Wales, his father, Morgan Dee, was 28 and his mother, Maud Day  Madoc, was -12. He married Margaret Day Howell in 1451, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1515, in Monmouthshire, Wales, at the age of 97, and was buried in Shinfield, Berkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Richard Day est né en 1418, à Denbighshire, Galles, son père, Morgan Dee, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Maud Day  Madoc, avait -12 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Day Howell en 1451, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1515, à Monmouthshire, Galles, à l’âge de 97 ans, et a été enterré à Shinfield, Berkshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Richard Day 1418 in Denbighshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Morgan Dee 28 und seine Mutter Maud Day  Madoc -12. Er hat 1451 in Shropshire, England Margaret Day Howell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1515 im Alter von 97 Jahren in Monmouthshire, Wales und wurde in Shinfield, Berkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Richard Day è nato il nel 1418 nel Denbighshire, Galles, il suo padre, Morgan Dee, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Maud Day  Madoc, aveva -12 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Day Howell nel 1451, nel Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1515, nel Monmouthshire, Galles, all'età di 97 anni, e fu sepolto a Shinfield, Berkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John Richard Dayが1418年にウェールズデンビグhシュアイアで生まれた時，父Morgan Deeは28歳，母Maud Day  Madocは-12歳であった。1451年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでMargaret Day Howellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1515年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアで97歳で死去し，イギリスバークシュアイアシュインフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Richard Day(이)가 1418년 웨일스 덴비그h실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Morgan Dee(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Day  Madoc(은)는 -12세였다. 그는 1451년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Margaret Day Howell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1515년 웨일스 몬모우스실르에서 97세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 버크실르 신필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Richard Day nasceu em 1418, em Denbighshire, Gales, seu pai, Morgan Dee, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Maud Day  Madoc, tinha -12 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Day Howell em 1451, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1515, em Monmouthshire, Gales, com 97 anos, e foi sepultado em Shinfield, Berkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Richard Day родился в 1418 г. в местности денбаишир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Morgan Dee было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Day  Madoc, -12. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Day Howell в 1451 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1515 г. в местности монмоутшир, Уэльс в возрасте 97 лет и был похоронен в местности синфиелд, биркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Richard Day nació en 1418, en Denbighshire, Gales, su papá, Morgan Dee, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Maud Day  Madoc, tenía -12 años. Se casó con Margaret Day Howell en 1451, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1515, en Monmouthshire, Gales, a la edad de 97 años, y fue sepultado en Shinfield, Berkshire, Inglaterra.
